page.content: The data table presents a comprehensive analysis of the willingness to use Electron Beam Lithography (EBL) among various stakeholders in the field of advanced manufacturing.

stakeholders in the field of advanced manufacturing.

page.html:
TechniqueAdvantagesLimitationsHighest Resolution
Electron beam lithography (EBL)Maskless, Stampless, High-resolution, Arbitrary patterning with different shapes and sizesSlow (serial process), Complicated, Expensive (Requiring equipment, clean room and vacuum condition), Small area patterning∼30 nm [89]
Nanocontact printing (NCP)Simple (direct patterning), Parallel, Cheap, Fast process, Large area patterningPreparing nanoscale stamp with high feature density, Mechanical stability of stamp, Diffusion of SAM inks∼70 nm [90]
Nanoimprint lithography (NIL)Large area patterning with a high-throughput and low-cost, ParallelStress and wear of mold, Use of polymer, Slow (molding, demolding, and etching process)∼75 nm [91]
Nanografting/ NanoshavingHigh-resolution, Ambient, Quick change of fabricated patternsSmall area patterning∼10 nm [92]
Dip-pen nanolithography (DPN)High-resolution, Ambient, Variety of inks usable, Parallelization possibleSlow (serial process), Small area patterning∼30 nm [93]

page.content: With a total sample size (N) of 155, the mean willingness score is recorded at 0.676, accompanied by a standard error (SE) of 0.063, indicating a moderate inclination towards the adoption of this technique. The confidence interval (CI) further elucidates the precision of this mean, providing critical insights into the variability of responses. EBL is recognized for its maskless and stampless capabilities, allowing for high-resolution patterning and the creation of arbitrary shapes and sizes, which are significant advantages in precision engineering. However, the technique also presents notable limitations, including a slow serial processing speed, complexity in operation, and high costs associated with the necessary equipment, cleanroom environments, and vacuum conditions. Understanding these factors is essential for stakeholders considering the integration of EBL into their manufacturing processes, as they balance the benefits against the operational challenges.
